hero.headline: Traceability infrastructure anyone in the chain can start using.
hero.subheadline: Tracebud gives farmers, cooperatives, exporters, and buyers a shared system to capture origin data once, govern how it is shared, preserve provenance through the chain, and generate reusable EUDR-ready compliance records.
hero.supportingText: Free for producers. Self-serve from the first request. Built for low-connectivity field realities and large, fragmented networks.
hero.subheadline: Tracebud gives farmers, cooperatives, exporters, and buyers one shared system for origin data.
hero.subheadlineSecondary: Capture data once, control how it is shared, preserve provenance through the chain, and generate reusable EUDR-ready compliance records.
hero.supportingText: Built for fragmented networks and low-connectivity field realities.

problemSection.headline: Compliance should be easy to start, but strong enough to trust.
problemSection.description: Most supply chains do not fail because people do not care. They fail because origin data, evidence, and shipment records are fragmented across actors and tools. Tracebud creates a shared system of record so compliance data can move through the network without losing context or provenance.

whyTracebudSection.headline: Because the best infrastructure is the kind people can actually start using.
whyTracebudSection.intro: Reduce manual follow-up. Preserve provenance through aggregation. Move with more confidence.

simple.title: Self-serve by design
simple.description: Anyone can join, start a request, or begin capture without consultants or complex setup.

automated.title: Inclusive by default
automated.description: Farmers can participate directly, even with limited digital access, shared devices, or spotty signal.

inclusive.title: Infrastructure underneath
inclusive.description: The data model, lineage, consent, and compliance records stay structured, reusable, and audit-ready.

howItWorksSection.headline: Four steps. Self-serve from the start.
howItWorksSection.subtitle: From first request to reusable compliance records.

step1.title: Add your network
step1.description: One contact or five hundred. Upload a CSV or add them manually.

step2.title: Send one request
step2.description: Let it flow through the chain automatically, so the right request reaches the right actor without manual chasing.

step3.title: Collect origin data once
step3.description: Capture producer, plot, evidence, and consent data once, then reuse it across shipments and buyers.

step4.title: Stay aligned
step4.description: Keep everyone on the same page with a shared workflow and a structured source of truth.

productsSection.headline: Two products, one shared operating layer.
productsSection.intro: Built for how fragmented supply chains actually work.

mobileApp.headline: Field capture that feeds the whole network.
mobileApp.description: Tracebud's mobile app is built for real sourcing conditions. Farmers and field teams can map plots, capture evidence, record consent, and respond through a simple mobile experience designed for low-connectivity environments. The result is structured origin data the whole chain can rely on.
mobileApp.supportingText: Free for producers. No training-heavy setup.

dashboard.headline: Network infrastructure, not spreadsheet orchestration.
dashboard.description: Upload contacts in bulk, send one request, and let the workflow move through your supply chain to source. Track readiness, identify missing data, and assemble provenance-linked compliance records from one shared operating layer.
dashboard.supportingText: Less chasing. Fewer gaps. Faster handoffs.

inclusionSection.headline: Compliance should not leave smallholders behind.
inclusionSection.description: Big companies can buy complex systems. Smallholders should not be excluded because the tools are too expensive, too technical, or too slow. Tracebud keeps every actor visible, connected, and able to participate, even in low-connectivity field conditions or shared-device environments.
inclusionSection.punchline: We remove the access barriers that kept smallholders out.

finalCtaSection.headline: Make compliance usable for the whole chain.
finalCtaSection.description: Start with the network you already have, and give every actor a way to participate. Tracebud makes it possible to begin self-serve, capture data once, and reuse it across shipments, buyers, and downstream workflows without turning the process into paperwork theater.
Start with the network you already have, and give every actor a way to participate — without turning the process into paperwork theater.

trustAndDataSection.headline: Compliance data should stay clear, controlled, and auditable.
trustAndDataSection.description: Producers can share data with specific organizations through consent-based workflows. Organizations can manage who sees what while preserving auditability and a reusable record of origin data.

goal.title: The goal
goal.description: Reduce repeated data collection without losing control of source records or the chain of custody.

whoItIsForSection.headline: Built for every link in the chain
whoItIsForSection.description: From farm to final buyer, every actor gets the tools they need.

producers.title: Producers
producers.description: Share farm and plot data simply, with no paperwork.

cooperatives.title: Cooperatives
cooperatives.description: Manage hundreds of members and requests in one place.

exporters.title: Exporters
exporters.description: Generate reliable shipment evidence, ready for audit.

buyers.title: Buyers
buyers.description: Access clean upstream data with no missing records.

regenerativeFarmingSection.label: Supporting change
regenerativeFarmingSection.headline: Supporting regenerative farming, not just compliance.
regenerativeFarmingSection.description: Tracebud gives supply chains a way to track and verify farm practices over time. That makes it easier to support regenerative transitions, document progress, and connect better data with better incentives.
regenerativeFarmingSection.benefit: When farmers can show what is happening on the ground, they are better positioned to access support, build trust, and improve long-term resilience.

pricingSection.headline: Simple monthly pricing by network size.

faq.title: Questions? We have answers.
faq.whenLaunch: When can I start using Tracebud?
faq.whenLaunchAnswer: Tracebud is opening in phases by region and commodity. Join the waitlist and we will email you when your use case is supported.
faq.whyCommoditiesAnswer: Coffee first, then the other six EUDR commodities: cocoa, palm oil, rubber, soy, cattle, and timber. We're starting where smallholder complexity meets immediate buyer demand.
faq.howLongAnswer: Producers can start mapping immediately with the offline app. Most cooperatives have their network mapped within a week. The dashboard is ready the moment you sign up.
faq.offlineCapabilityAnswer: Yes. The mobile app works completely offline. Producers map plots, share information, and respond to requests without a connection. Data syncs automatically once back online.
faq.dataControlAnswer: You stay in control. Producers choose which organizations they share data with. Organizations can manage permissions across their team. Everything is logged with a full audit trail for compliance.
faq.regenerativeFarmingAnswer: Yes. Tracebud lets you track and verify farm practices over time. That makes it easier to document progress, support transitions, and connect better data with better incentives for regenerative farming.
faq.whoItIsForAnswer: No. Tracebud helps cooperatives manage many members, exporters gather reliable shipment evidence, and buyers get cleaner upstream data. Everyone in the chain can participate, even with limited digital access.
faq.whyCommoditiesAnswer: Coffee first, then the other six EUDR commodities: cocoa, palm oil, rubber, soy, cattle, and timber. See our pricing page for the full roadmap.
faq.howCostAnswer: Free for producers. Cooperatives, exporters, and buyers pay based on network size and usage. Visit the pricing page or join the waitlist for details.
faq.howLongAnswer: Producers can start mapping with the offline app right away. Most cooperatives upload their network and send the first request within a week.
faq.supportAnswer: Every waitlist member gets a personal follow-up by email. Once onboarded, you have direct access to our team for setup and compliance questions.
